signals.title: AUD/USD — Geopolitical + Positioning + Retail
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7257 testing the 0.7260 region as Trump rejects the Iran deal, driving a 3.13% surge in WTI Oil ($98.4). AUD COT LF net long +58,994 (78.3% long) indicates heavy institutional backing, while retail sentiment is 89% short. This extreme institutional vs retail divergence suggests a high-conviction trend; retail capitulation risk accelerates if price clears 0.7260. RBA 4.35% vs Fed 3.75% provides a +60bp carry tailwind for AUD.

signals.title: GBP/JPY — G8 Bilateral Theme + Carry
signals.text: GBP/JPY at 214.342 reflects a dominant G8 bilateral theme as GBP outperforms (+0.505%) and JPY lags (-0.542%). BoE 3.75% vs BoJ 0.75% provides a +300bp carry premium, supporting the pair in a normal volatility regime (VIX 18.1). JPY COT LF net short -61,340 shows crowded carry positioning, leaving the pair vulnerable only if VIX spikes above 25. Watch for BoJ verbal intervention if JPY weakness accelerates toward the next psychological level.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + RBNZ
signals.text: NZD/USD COT LF net short -19,294 (long% at 18.5% extreme) shows speculators heavily fading the currency despite a hawkish RBNZ market bias (Hike). With the RBNZ meeting on 27 May, a hawkish delivery or signal triggers a significant short-squeeze risk as crowded speculative shorts unwind. NZD/USD at 0.5967 remains supported by the broader commodity bid despite the +150bp Fed-RBNZ carry deficit.

signals.title: USD/CHF — Technical + Retail Divergence
signals.text: USD/CHF at 0.7777 holding below the 100-hour moving average as key resistance. Retail sentiment is 87% long, diverging from the COT LF net short of -7,392. This extreme retail long positioning against institutional shorts suggests further downside pressure; a failure to reclaim the 100-hour MA targets a move toward 0.7750. Fed 3.75% vs SNB 0% (+375bp) carry is currently secondary to technical resistance and safe-haven CHF demand amid Iran tensions.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Correlation Break + Yields
signals.text: USD/JPY vs US 10Y correlation has shifted to +0.642 (z=-2.08σ from 0.153 norm), indicating the pair is now hyper-sensitive to US Treasury moves rather than broader risk sentiment. US 10Y at 4.404% (+0.92%) keeps USD/JPY bid at 157.12. Fed 3.75% vs BoJ 0.75% (+300bp) carry remains the structural floor. Watch US CPI on Wednesday to confirm if the yield-driven bid extends toward 158.00.
